name: angles
description:
This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

name: class_loader
description:
The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library.
    class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of these exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

name: control_msgs
description:
control_msgs contains base messages and actions useful for
    controlling robots.  It provides representations for controller
    setpoints and joint and cartesian trajectories.

name: ecl_command_line
description:
Embeds the TCLAP library inside the ecl. This is a very convenient
     command line parser in templatised c++.

name: ecl_concepts
description:
Introduces a compile time concept checking mechanism that can be used
     most commonly to check for required functionality when passing
     template arguments.

name: ecl_containers
description:
The containers included here are intended to extend the stl containers.
    In all cases, these implementations are designed to implement
    c++ conveniences and safety where speed is not sacrificed.

    Also includes techniques for memory debugging of common problems such
    as buffer overruns.

name: ecl_converters
description:
Some fast/convenient type converters, mostly for char strings or strings.
     These are not really fully fleshed out, alot of them could use the addition for
     the whole range of fundamental types (e.g. all integers, not just int, unsigned int).

     They will come as the need arises.

name: ecl_core
description:
A set of tools and interfaces extending the capabilities of c++ to
    provide a lightweight, consistent interface with a focus for control
    programming.

name: ecl_core_apps
description:
This includes a suite of programs demo'ing various aspects of the
     ecl_core. It also includes various benchmarking and utility programs for
     use primarily with embedded systems.

name: ecl_devices
description: Provides an extensible and standardised framework for input-output devices.

name: ecl_eigen
description: This provides an Eigen implementation for ecl's linear algebra.

name: ecl_exceptions
description:
Template based exceptions - these are simple and practical
     and avoid the proliferation of exception types. Although not
     syntatactically ideal, it is convenient and eminently practical.

name: ecl_filesystem
description: Cross platform filesystem utilities (until c++11 makes its way in).

name: ecl_formatters
description:
The formatters here simply format various input types to a specified
   text format. They can be used with most streaming types (including both
   ecl and stl streams).

name: ecl_geometry
description:
Any tools relating to mathematical geometry.
     Primarily featuring polynomials and interpolations.

name: ecl_ipc
description:
Interprocess mechanisms vary greatly across platforms - sysv, posix, win32, there
  are more than a few. This package provides an infrastructure to allow for developing
  cross platform c++ wrappers around the lower level c api's that handle these
  mechanisms. These make it not only easier to utilise such mechanisms, but allow it
  to be done consistently across platforms.

name: ecl_linear_algebra
description: Ecl frontend to a linear matrix package (currently eigen).

name: ecl_manipulators
description:
Deploys various manipulation algorithms, currently just
    feedforward filters (interpolations).

name: ecl_math
description:
This package provides simple support to cmath, filling in holes
    or redefining in a c++ formulation where desirable.

name: ecl_mobile_robot
description:
Contains transforms (e.g. differential drive inverse kinematics)
    for the various types of mobile robot platforms.

name: ecl_mpl
description:
Metaprogramming tools move alot of runtime calculations to be shifted to
    compile time. This has only very elementary structures at this stage.

name: ecl_sigslots
description:
Provides a signal/slot mechanism (in the same vein as qt sigslots,
     boost::signals etc for intra-process communication. These include
     some improvements - they do not need a preprocessor, are fully type safe,
     allow for simple connections via a posix style string identifier
     and are multithread-safe.

name: ecl_statistics
description: Common statistical structures and algorithms for control systems.

name: ecl_streams
description:
These are lightweight text streaming classes that connect to standardised
     ecl type devices.

name: ecl_threads
description:
This package provides the c++ extensions for a variety of threaded
     programming tools. These are usually different on different
     platforms, so the architecture for a cross-platform framework
     is also implemented.

name: ecl_time
description:
Timing utilities are very dependent on the system api provided for their use.
	This package provides a means for handling different timing models. Current support

	- posix rt : complete.
	- macosx : posix timers only, missing absolute timers.
	- win : none.

name: ecl_type_traits
description: Extends c++ type traits and implements a few more to boot.

name: ecl_utilities
description: Includes various supporting tools and utilities for c++ programming.

name: ecl_config
description:
These tools inspect and describe your system with macros, types
     and functions.

name: ecl_console
description: Color codes for ansii consoles.

name: ecl_converters_lite
description:
These are a very simple version of some of the functions in ecl_converters
     suitable for firmware development. That is, there is no use of new,
     templates or exceptions.

name: ecl_errors
description:
This library provides lean and mean error mechanisms.
    It includes c style error functions as well as a few
    useful macros. For higher level mechanisms,
    refer to ecl_exceptions.

name: ecl_io
description:
Most implementations (windows, posix, ...) have slightly different api for
     low level input-output functions. These are gathered here and re-represented
     with a cross platform set of functions.

name: ecl_lite
description: Libraries and utilities for embedded and low-level linux development.

name: ecl_sigslots_lite
description:
This avoids use of dynamic storage (malloc/new) and thread safety (mutexes) to
     provide a very simple sigslots implementation that can be used for *very*
     embedded development.

name: ecl_time_lite
description:
Provides a portable set of time functions that are especially useful for
     porting other code or being wrapped by higher level c++ classes.

name: ecl_build
description:
Collection of cmake/make build tools primarily for ecl development itself, but also
     contains a few cmake modules useful outside of the ecl.

name: ecl_license
description:
Maintains the ecl licenses and also provides an install
     target for deploying licenses with the ecl libraries.

name: ecl_tools
description: Tools and utilities for ecl development.

name: kdl_parser
description:
The Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) defines a tree structure
   to represent the kinematic and dynamic parameters of a robot
   mechanism.
authors: Wim Meeussen, Ioan Sucan, Jackie Kay

name: laser_geometry
description:
This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.
authors: Dave Hershberger, Mabel Zhang, Radu Bogdan Rusu, Tully Foote, William Woodall

name: map_msgs
description: This package defines messages commonly used in mapping packages.
maintainers: David V. Lu!!, Michael Ferguson, Steve Macenski

name: pluginlib
description:
The pluginlib package provides tools for writing and dynamically loading plugins using the ROS build infrastructure.
    To work, these tools require plugin providers to register their plugins in the package.xml of their package.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Tully Foote, Dirk Thomas, Mirza Shah

name: libcurl_vendor
description: Wrapper around libcurl, it provides a fixed CMake module and an ExternalProject build of it.

name: resource_retriever
description:
This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.
authors: Chris Lalancette, Ioan Sucan, Josh Faust, Shane Loretz

name: ros_environment
description: The package provides the environment variables `ROS_VERSION` and `ROS_DISTRO`.

name: sophus
description: C++ implementation of Lie Groups using Eigen.

name: ament_cmake
description: The entry point package for the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_auto
description: The auto-magic functions for ease to use of the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_core
description:
The core of the ament buildsystem in CMake.

    Several subcomponents provide specific funtionalities:
    * environment: provide prefix-level setup files
    * environment_hooks: provide package-level setup files and environment hooks
    * index: store information in an index and retrieve them without crawling
    * package_templates: templates from the ament_package Python package
    * symlink_install: use symlinks for CMake install commands

name: ament_cmake_export_definitions
description: The ability to export definitions to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem.

name: ament_cmake_export_dependencies
description: The ability to export dependencies to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_export_include_directories
description: The ability to export include directories to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_export_interfaces
description: The ability to export interfaces to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_export_libraries
description: The ability to export libraries to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_export_link_flags
description: The ability to export link flags to downstream packages in the ament buildsystem.

name: ament_cmake_gmock
description: The ability to add Google mock-based tests in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_gtest
description: The ability to add gtest-based tests in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_include_directories
description: The functionality to order include directories according to a chain of prefixes in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_libraries
description: The functionality to deduplicate libraries in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_nose
description: The ability to add nose-based tests in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_pytest
description: The ability to run Python tests using pytest in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_python
description: The ability to use Python in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_target_dependencies
description: The ability to add definitions, include directories and libraries of a package to a target in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_test
description: The ability to add tests in the ament buildsystem in CMake.

name: ament_cmake_ros
description: The ROS specific CMake bits in the ament buildsystem.

name: ament_index_cpp
description: C++ API to access the ament resource index.

name: ament_index_python
description: Python API to access the ament resource index.

name: ament_clang_format
description:
The ability to check code against style conventions using
    clang-format and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_cmake_clang_format
description: The CMake API for ament_clang_format to lint C / C++ code using clang format.

name: ament_cmake_copyright
description: The CMake API for ament_copyright to check every source file contains copyright reference.

name: ament_cmake_cppcheck
description:
The CMake API for ament_cppcheck to perform static code analysis on C/C++
    code using Cppcheck.

name: ament_cmake_cpplint
description: The CMake API for ament_cpplint to lint C / C++ code using cpplint.

name: ament_cmake_flake8
description:
The CMake API for ament_flake8 to check code syntax and style conventions
    with flake8.

name: ament_cmake_lint_cmake
description: The CMake API for ament_lint_cmake to lint CMake code using cmakelint.

name: ament_cmake_pclint
description:
The CMake API for ament_pclint to perform static code analysis on C/C++
    code using PCLint.

name: ament_cmake_pep257
description:
The CMake API for ament_pep257 to check code against the style conventions in
    PEP 257.

name: ament_cmake_pep8
description:
The CMake API for ament_pep8 to check code against the style conventions in
    PEP 8.

name: ament_cmake_pyflakes
description: The CMake API for ament_pyflakes to check code using pyflakes.

name: ament_cmake_uncrustify
description:
The CMake API for ament_uncrustify to check code against styleconventions
    using uncrustify.

name: ament_copyright
description:
The ability to check source files for copyright and license
    information.

name: ament_cppcheck
description:
The ability to perform static code analysis on C/C++ code using Cppcheck
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_cpplint
description:
The ability to check code against the Google style conventions using
    cpplint and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_flake8
description: The ability to check code for style and syntax conventions with flake8.

name: ament_lint_auto
description: The auto-magic functions for ease to use of the ament linters in CMake.

name: ament_lint_cmake
description:
The ability to lint CMake code using cmakelint and generate xUnit test
    result files.

name: ament_lint_common
description: The list of commonly used linters in the ament buildsytem in CMake.

name: ament_pclint
description:
The ability to perform static code analysis on C/C++ code using pclint
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pep257
description:
The ability to check code against the style conventions in PEP 8 and
    generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pep8
description:
The ability to check code against the style conventions in PEP 8 and
    generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_pyflakes

The ability to check code using pyflakes and generate xUnit test
    result files.

name: ament_uncrustify

The ability to check code against style conventions using uncrustify
    and generate xUnit test result files.

name: ament_package
description: The parser for the manifest files in the ament buildsystem.

name: cartographer
description:
Cartographer is a system that provides real-time simultaneous localization
    and mapping (SLAM) in 2D and 3D across multiple platforms and sensor
    configurations.

name: cartographer_ros
description:
Cartographer is a system that provides real-time simultaneous localization
    and mapping (SLAM) in 2D and 3D across multiple platforms and sensor
    configurations. This package provides Cartographer's ROS integration.

name: cartographer_ros_msgs
description: ROS messages for the cartographer_ros package.

name: actionlib_msgs
description: A package containing some message definitions used in the implementation or actions.

name: common_interfaces
description: common_interfaces contains messages and services that are widely used by other ROS packages.

name: diagnostic_msgs
description: A package containing some diagnostics related message and service definitions.

name: geometry_msgs
description: A package containing some geometry related message definitions.

name: nav_msgs
description: A package containing some navigation related message and service definitions.

name: sensor_msgs
description: A package containing some sensor data related message and service definitions.

name: shape_msgs
description: A package containing some message definitions which describe geometric shapes.

name: std_msgs
description: A package containing some standard message definitions.

name: std_srvs
description: A package containing some standard service definitions.

name: stereo_msgs
description: A package containing some stereo camera related message definitions.

name: trajectory_msgs
description: A package containing some robot trajectory message definitions.

name: visualization_msgs
description: A package containing some visualization and interaction related message definitions.

name: composition
description: Examples for composing multiple nodes in a single process.

name: demo_nodes_cpp
description: C++ nodes which were previously in the ros2/examples repository but are now just used for demo purposes.

name: demo_nodes_cpp_native
description: C++ nodes which access the native handles of the rmw implemenation.

name: demo_nodes_py
description: Python nodes which were previously in the ros2/examples repository but are now just used for demo purposes.

name: dummy_map_server
description: dummy map server node

name: dummy_robot_bringup
description: dummy robot bringup

name: dummy_sensors
description: dummy sensor nodes

name: image_tools
description: Tools to capture and play back images to and from DDS subscriptions and publications.

name: intra_process_demo
description: Demonstrations of intra process communication.

name: lifecycle
description: Package containing demos for lifecycle implementation

name: logging_demo
description: Examples for using and configuring loggers.

name: pendulum_control
description: Demonstrates ROS 2's realtime capabilities with a simulated inverted pendulum.

name: pendulum_msgs
description: Custom messages for real-time pendulum control.

name: topic_monitor
description: Package containing tools for monitoring ROS 2 topics.

name: example_interfaces
description: Contains message and service definitions used by the examples.

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_client
description: Examples of minimal service clients

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_composition
description:
Minimalist examples of composing nodes in the same
  process

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_publisher
description: Examples of minimal publisher nodes

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_service
description: A minimal service server which adds two numbers

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_subscriber
description: Examples of minimal subscribers

name: examples_rclcpp_minimal_timer
description: Examples of minimal nodes which have timers

name: examples_rclpy_executors
description: Examples of creating and using exectors to run multiple nodes in the same process

name: examples_rclpy_minimal_client
description: Examples of minimal service clients using rclpy.

name: examples_rclpy_minimal_service
description: Examples of minimal service servers using rclpy.

name: examples_rclpy_minimal_publisher
description: Examples of minimal publishers using rclpy.

name: examples_rclpy_minimal_subscriber
description: Examples of minimal subscribers using rclpy.

name: fastcdr
description: *eProsima Fast CDR* is a C++ serialization library implementing the Common Data Representation (CDR) mechanism defined by the Object Management Group (OMG) consortium. CDR is the serialization mechanism used in DDS for the DDS Interoperability Wire Protocol (DDSI-RTPS).

name: tf2
description:
tf2 is the second generation of the transform library, which lets
    the user keep track of multiple coordinate frames over time. tf2
    maintains the relationship between coordinate frames in a tree
    structure buffered in time, and lets the user transform points,
    vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at any desired
    point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

name: tf2_eigen
description: tf2_eigen

name: tf2_geometry_msgs
description: tf2_geometry_msgs

name: tf2_msgs
description: tf2_msgs

name: tf2_ros
description: This package contains the ROS bindings for the tf2 library, for both Python and C++.

name: gmock_vendor
description: The package provides GoogleMock.
authors: Audrow Nash, Dirk Thomas, Michel Hidalgo

name: gtest_vendor
description: The package provides GoogleTest.
authors: Audrow Nash, Dirk Thomas, Michel Hidalgo

name: launch
description: The ROS launch tool.

name: launch_ros
description: ROS specific extensions to the launch tool.

name: launch_testing
description: Helper scripts for tests that use the ROS launch tool.

name: ros2launch
description: The launch command for ROS 2 command line tools.

name: libyaml_vendor
description: Wrapper around libyaml, provides the last version (1.8.0 rc) that ships with a CMake module

name: osrf_pycommon

name: test_osrf_testing_tools_cpp

name: rcl
description:
The ROS client library common implementation.
    This package contains an API which builds on the ROS middleware API and is optionally built upon by the other ROS client libraries.

name: rcl_lifecycle
description: Package containing a C-based lifecycle implementation

name: rcl_yaml_param_parser
description: Package containing various utility types and functions for C

name: builtin_interfaces

name: lifecycle_msgs

name: rcl_interfaces
description:
The ROS client library common interfaces.
    This package contains the messages and services which ROS client libraries will use under the hood to
    communicate higher level concepts such as parameters.

name: rosgraph_msgs
description:
Messages relating to the ROS Computation Graph.
    These are generally considered to be low-level messages that end users do not interact with.

name: test_msgs
description: A package containing message definitions and fixtures used exclusively for testing purposes.

name: rclcpp
description: The ROS client library in C++.

name: rclcpp_lifecycle

name: rclpy

name: rcutils

name: rttest

authors: Dave Hershberger, David Gossow, Josh Faust

Wrapper around assimp, providing nothing but a dependency on assimp, on some systems.
    On others, it provides a fixed CMake module or even an ExternalProject build of assimp.

authors: Dave Hershberger, David Gossow, Josh Faust

name: rviz_visual_testing_framework

name: sros2
description: Command line tools for managing SROS2 keys

name: teleop_twist_joy

name: teleop_twist_keyboard

A robot-agnostic teleoperation node to convert keyboard commands to Twist

Wrapper around tinyxml2, providing nothing but a dependency on tinyxml2, on some systems.

name: urdf

This package contains a C++ parser for the Unified Robot Description
    Format (URDF), which is an XML format for representing a robot model.
    The code API of the parser has been through our review process and will remain
    backwards compatible in future releases.

name: desktop
description: A package which extends 'ros_base' and includes high level packages like vizualization tools and demos.

name: ros_base
description: A package which extends 'ros_core' and includes other basic functionalities like tf2 and urdf.

name: ros_core
description: A package to aggregate the packages required to use publish / subscribe, services, generate messages and other core ROS concepts.

name: orocos_kdl
description:
This package contains a recent version of the Kinematics and Dynamics
    Library (KDL), distributed by the Orocos Project.

name: rmw_connext_cpp
description: Implement the ROS middleware interface using RTI Connext static code generation in C++.

name: rmw_connext_shared_cpp
description: C++ types and functions shared by the ROS middleware interface to RTI Connext Static and RTI Connext Dynamic.

name: connext_cmake_module
description: Provide CMake module to find RTI Connext.

name: rosidl_typesupport_connext_c
description: Generate the C interfaces for RTI Connext.

name: rosidl_typesupport_connext_cpp
description: Generate the C++ interfaces for RTI Connext.

name: joy
description:
ROS2 driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    Will contain a MacOS and Windows version later.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS2. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend, Jonathan Bohren

name: poco_vendor
description: CMake shim over the poco library.

name: rmw_opensplice_cpp
description: Implement the ROS middleware interface using PrismTech OpenSplice static code generation in C++.

name: opensplice_cmake_module
description: Provide CMake module to find PrismTech OpenSplice.

name: rosidl_typesupport_opensplice_c
